analysis.name: orders_2024_messy.csv
analysis.dataset_description: This file appears to contain a list of orders, including order IDs, customer IDs, total costs, dates, and statuses.

column_overview.name: CustID
column_overview.inferred_role: Identifier for the customer who made the order

column_overview.name: Total
column_overview.inferred_role: The total cost of the order

column_overview.name: OrderDate
column_overview.inferred_role: The date when the order was placed
column_overview.notes: Observed inconsistent date formats: YYYY-MM-DD and MM/DD/YYYY. This may cause issues with date-based analysis or sorting.

column_overview.name: Status
column_overview.inferred_role: The status of the order (e.g., completed, pending)
column_overview.notes: Observed inconsistent capitalization in status values (e.g., 'completed', 'Complete', 'DONE'). This may cause issues with case-sensitive analysis or filtering.

analysis.summary: This file contains 20 sample rows and appears to be a list of orders. However, it has some formatting inconsistencies that may require attention.
key_problems: Inconsistent date formats in the OrderDate column
Inconsistent capitalization in the Status column
recommendations: Standardize the date format in the OrderDate column to a consistent format (e.g., YYYY-MM-DD)
Normalize the capitalization in the Status column to a consistent case (e.g., lowercase)
confidence_notes: The sample size is relatively small, and further analysis may reveal additional issues or inconsistencies.
